extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r4, which requires the full 8-way conjunction (hypertension AND ckd_dialysis AND bradycardia AND polypharmacy AND pregnancy AND hyperkalemia AND g6pd_deficiency AND elderly); no other rule touches rec_drug_a, and the default is false. Therefore rec_drug_a = true ONLY when that full conjunction holds. In that exact input region, rec_drug_b is determined by two matching rules: r2 (priority 50, hypertension is true here) sets it true, and r6 (priority 90, full conjunction true) sets it false. Conflict resolution is per-output highest-priority-wins, so r6 (90) beats r2 (50) and forces rec_drug_b := false. No other rule can set rec_drug_b true. Hence whenever rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is false, so (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is never jointly true. The mutual-exclusion property holds across the entire input space.
